results.title: Patrick
results.creator_credit: Gene Deitch, Morton Schindel, Kratky Film and Weston Woods Studios
results.brief_description: An animated cartoon for children which tells the story of Patrick and of the magical things that happen when he plays his violin. Based on the book written and illustrated by Quentin Blake, and told without words. Sound montage by Gene Deitch. Animation by Milan Klikar and Bohumil Sejda. Colour and background paintings by Miluse Hluchanicova.

results.title: Pathways to the Future Worker Co-Operatives in N.S.W.
results.brief_description: This film shows how worker co-operatives are established and maintained at both financial and managerial levels. The benefits to participants in the co-operative movement is also demonstrated

results.title: Pathway into light
results.creator_credit: Terry Ashwood, Howard Thomas and Royal National Institute for the Blind
results.brief_description: Parisian tributes to Louis de Braille are shown as well as the opening of his Centenary Exhibition by the Duke of Edinburgh, early Braille punching machines, modern production methods, the library of the Royal National Institute for the Blind and various teaching methods. Commentary written by Lambton Burn and spoken by Jack Hawkins and Jean Metcalfe.

results.title: Paths to progress
results.brief_description: In Chiang Mai, the capital of Thailand, the Australian Development Assistance Bureau is working with the University’s Faculty of Agriculture in a highland Ergonomie project to help develop agricultural potential for some 80% of the Thai population living in small villages.

results.title: Pathe Pictorial: The Week in pictures. Issue no. 291
results.brief_description: Features a judo demonstration, pioneers English tobacco growing, modelling new fashion garments, and music at a London club. From the series “The Week in pictures”.

results.title: Pathe pictorial goes to sea
results.brief_description: Waves breaking in the sea. Old ships; cadets in training on sailing ships. Knot tying. Schooling on a trawler at Grimbsy. Throwing nets. Fishing boats and nets a shore. Old seaman finding a bottle with a message from a ship-wrecked man to his wife - he goes to the city and looks for her (shown as an incongruous figure out of place in the city). Finds only ruins.

results.title: Pathe news: Sierra Leone independence
results.creator_credit: Associated British-Pathe and British Information Services
results.brief_description: The ceremonies and celebrations preceding Independence Day in Sierra Leone, and events in the capital city of Freetown on the day of inauguration. HRH the Duke of Kent, representing Her Majesty the Queen, is seen attending a party at Government House, and speaking at the State Opening of the Sierra Leone Parliament. A water regatta and displays of traditional dancing are among the activities enjoyed by the people of Sierra Leone.

results.title: Pathe news: Shakespeare's 400th anniversary
results.brief_description: Events at Stratford-upon-Avon to commemorate the four hundredth birthday of William Shakespeare. Celebrations included the opening of the new Shakespeare Centre, and a procession of local dignitaries with representatives from 115 nations, to lay wreaths on the poet’s tomb at Holy Trinity Church. H.R.H the Duke of Edinburgh arrived to pay tribute, visiting Shakespeare Centre the cottage where the poet was born, and the Royal Shakespeare Memorial Theatre on the banks of the Avon.

results.title: Pathe's animated gazette. No. 346
results.brief_description: This film is made up of the following items: leading Paris fashions; Trafalgar day march; Corporal Bassett, the first New Zealander to win the VC; the destroyed town of Mount St. Eloy; Sir Very Strong visits Bethnal Green military hospital; training recruits in mining and bombing attacks; ammunitions factories; inspection of French artillery; exhibition of German weapons.
